paper_title: Can LLM be a Personalized Judge?

procedure: Present LLM with subjective question and two distinct responses
LLM infers preference of real user based on user persona
LLM estimates certainty level in prediction
Compare inferred preference against user's self-reported ground truth
methodology.identified_issue: Persona sparsity issue - persona variables may not offer informed prior for specific tasks
methodology.proposed_solution: Verbal uncertainty estimation component to assign lower certainty to samples with insufficient predictive power

key_findings.result: Only around 70% agreement with human judgments in binary choice scenarios, dropping below 60% for certain tasks
Identified as major factor contributing to unreliability
Achieves above 80% performance in high-certainty samples
LLM-as-a-Personalized-Judge achieves performance comparable to third-person human evaluation

context.problem_statement: Current alignment process assumes homogeneous human preferences, ignoring individual perspectives in subjective tasks
context.related_work: Previous work on fine-tuning LLMs for individual preferences and role-playing capabilities
context.limitation_of_existing_approaches: MT-Bench includes role-playing but only considers simplistic personas without addressing complex personas encompassing demographics, user descriptions, and prior interactions

title: Can LLM be a Personalized Judge?
